pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.questionText: Organisms that are r-selected (r-strategists) are able to colonise a habitat rapidly, utilising the food and other resources before other organisms are established and begin to compete. The r-strategists tend to be relatively small organisms with short life spans (e.g., bacteria) and often live in temporary or unstable environments; characteristically their survival depends on their ability to produce large numbers of offspring rather than on their ability to compete.

The protoplast of the male gametophyte divides mitotically to produce two unequal cells — a small generative cell and a large vegetative cell. The generative cell divides later into two non-motile male gametes (or sperms). Thus, the male gametophyte in angiosperms produces two sperms and a vegetative cell. The vegetative cell, later on, grows to produce pollen tube.

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.questionText: Sea anemone gets associated to the shell of hermit crab. It provides camouflage and protection to the crab due to presence of stinging cells in sea anemone. In turn, sea anemone is transported to new places reaching new food sources. This is symbiosis as both the organisms are benefitted.
pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.explanation: Sea anemone and hermit crab show symbiosis (mutualism). The sea anemone provides camouflage and protection to the hermit crab through its stinging cells, while the sea anemone is carried to new places and gets access to new food sources. Thus, both organisms benefit.

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.explanation: Tropical forests are found in the tropical zone of the world and are characterised by very high temperature with abundant rainfall. Their flora is rich and highly diversified, and the fauna is also very rich in density and variety. Because of the occurrence of suitable environmental conditions, these forests support high diversity and are more vulnerable to invasion by outside plants and animals.

Pollen-pistil interaction is the group of events that occur from the time of pollen deposition over the stigma to the time of pollen tube entry into ovule. It is a safety measure to ensure that illegitimate crossing does not occur.
pyq_sexual-reproduction-in-flowering-pla.explanation: Pollen grains of a number of plants may settle over a stigma. The pollens belonging to the same species would germinate while others fail to do so, but the pollen tube of the compatible pollen will grow through the style to reach the ovule whereas growth of incompatible pollens will be arrested at the stigmatic disc or sometimes in the beginning part of style.

pyq_sexual-reproduction-in-flowering-pla.questionText: [DIAGRAM: A simple schematic with two parallel horizontal lines, each ending in solid black circles at both ends.] Formation of gametophyte directly from sporophyte without meiosis and spore formation is called:
pyq_sexual-reproduction-in-flowering-pla.explanation: Formation of gametophyte directly from sporophyte without meiosis and spore formation is apospory. The gametophyte thus has diploid number of chromosomes. Such gametophyte may form viable gametes which fuse to form tetraploid sporophyte. Apogamy is development of sporophyte directly from gametophytic tissue without fusion of gametes. Amphimixis is normal sexual reproduction. Parthenogenesis is development of embryo from egg without fertilisation.
pyq_sexual-reproduction-in-flowering-pla.imageSummary: A simple schematic shows two parallel horizontal lines with solid black circles at both ends.

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.questionText: Fertility of soil is measured by its ability to
Based on the graph, which population is more successful in competing for limited resources?

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.explanation: Both the populations are herbivorous, thus they cannot affect each other. If the food sources for these populations A and B have decreased, then both the populations A and B would have declined. If population A have produced more offspring then the graph A should have increased. Based on the graph, population B is more successful in competing with population A, that is why number of organisms in population B increased while that in population A decreased, as they get access to limited resources.

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.questionText: In mutualism or symbiosis both the organisms in association are mutually benefitted and further this association is obligatory, i.e., necessary for existence of both organisms. Mycorrhiza is an example of symbiosis. It is association between roots of higher plants and fungal hyphae. The fungal hyphae supply water and nutrients to the plant and in turn get food from the plant. So both the organisms are mutually benefitted.

pyq_organisms-and-populations-pdf_120933.questionText:
Consider the following four statements (1-4) about certain desert animals such as kangaroo rat.
(1) They have dark colour and high rate of reproduction and excrete solid urine.
(2) They do not drink water, breathe at a slow rate to conserve water and have their body covered with thick hairs.
(3) They feed on dry seeds and do not require drinking water.
(4) They excrete very concentrated urine and do not use water to regulate body temperature.
Which two of the above statements for such animals are true?

pyq_sexual-reproduction-in-flowering-pla.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not true?

options.text: Pollen grains of many species cause severe allergies.
Stored pollen in liquid nitrogen can be used in the crop breeding programmes.
Tapetum helps in the dehiscence of anther.
Exine of pollen grains is made up of sporopollenin.
